compiled_state.title: Corrupt ticket c364-5a18 missing ticket_type field causes ticket list consumers to crash
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description: ticket c364-5a18 has only keys [ticket_id, status, error] — missing ticket_type, title, and other required fields. Any consumer iterating ticket list and accessing ticket_type without .get() will KeyError. The reducer should either exclude errored tickets from list output or ensure all tickets have the required schema fields.

comments.body: Scope clarification: fix should not just correct the single corrupt ticket c364-5a18 — it should add schema validation to the reducer so that tickets with missing required fields (ticket_type, title, status, etc.) are either rejected at write time or excluded/flagged in list output. Preventive, not just corrective.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | Data/code bug: corrupt ticket missing fields. Fix requires editing plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-reducer.py (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Severity=1 (functional degradation), Complexity=0 (single file), Environment=0 (local)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Investigation RESULT: ROOT_CAUSE: reduce_all_tickets() in ticket-reducer.py produces 3-key error dicts ({ticket_id, status, error}) for empty ticket dirs — missing ticket_type, title, and all standard fields. No current consumer crashes (all use .get()), but error-state dicts flow unfiltered through ticket-list.sh output. CONFIDENCE: high. FIX: (1) Add minimum schema fields to error-state dicts in reduce_all_tickets(), (2) Filter error/fsck tickets from ticket-list.sh default output.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
